run_summary: Screen 1 faces a basket-rule scenario: 10 fresh flags across two distinct triggers (Android 17 Gemini + Meta AI Mode), with 6 flags at conf 4 and 4 at conf 3, all OVERDONE with unjustified panic. Portfolio already holds 6 positions (all from earlier Anthropic-related triggers) at days 6–7, with mixed P&L. New positions must respect the basket rule and holding-window discipline.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: Process-control metrology for advanced nodes is a direct beneficiary of AI-chip manufacturing complexity — more compute = smaller geometries = more inspection passes per wafer. A Gemini-on-Android release has no mechanical link to wafer fab inspection demand. The sharp drop on light volume looks like ETF-driven semi-cap sympathy with an already-extended five-day base.
entries.what_kills: Memory market downturn affecting Onto's specialty-semi exposure; delay or unfavorable terms on the Rigaku transaction; broader semi-cap capex air pocket.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Conf 4, OVERDONE, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Onto Innovation (process-control metrology for advanced semiconductor nodes) is a direct beneficiary of AI chip complexity—smaller geometries, more compute = more wafer inspection passes. Android 17 Gemini release has zero mechanical link to fab metrology demand. Five-day move +15.37% before today's -5.9% shows sympathy overshoot on light volume (0.75x). Filings evidence clean: core moat is inspection tech, not end-market AI features. Part of Android 17 basket (3+ flags from same trigger); basket rule applies → exploratory tier.

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day +15.37% move combined with today's -5.9% reversal on 0.75x volume could represent real earnings-cycle risk or guidance revisions, not merely sympathy overshoot.
red_team.critique: Haiku frames this as pure Android/Gemini sympathy fade, but the setup description notes 'sharp drop on light volume looks like ETF-driven semi-cap sympathy with an already-extended five-day base'—that already-extended characterization undermines the bull thesis that the move was irrational. A +15% five-day rally followed by a -5.9% one-day reversal on sub-average volume is consistent with either short-covering unwind or pre-earnings position trimming, not confidence in the core metrology thesis. Haiku provides no earnings date, guidance revision, or peer weakness data to rule out deteriorating fab capex sentiment within the semiconductor metrology subsector itself.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: AEIS's own 10-K identifies AI infrastructure as a material growth driver. Power conversion for fabs and AI data centers is structurally long the buildout that Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude inference all require. A consumer OS release has zero mechanical impact on data-center power-delivery demand. The sympathy drop on weak volume with a still-positive five-day looks like a clean overshoot.
entries.what_kills: Hyperscaler capex digestion narrative gaining traction; loss of design-win share to a competitor; Data Center Computing margins continuing to compress as that segment grows.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Conf 4, OVERDONE, threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Advanced Energy Industries: own 10-K explicitly IDs AI infrastructure as revenue tailwind ('substantially increased' demand). Power conversion for fabs and data centers is structurally long the AI capex cycle—Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude all require data-center infrastructure AEIS powers. Android consumer OS release has zero mechanical impact on data-center power-delivery. Five-day still +12.45% despite today's -5.45% move—clean overshoot. Same Android basket; exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The company's 10-K disclosure of AI infrastructure as a tailwind is a lagging indicator if hyperscaler capex digestion narratives are already circulating in Q2 2026 earnings calls.
red_team.critique: Haiku correctly identifies that a consumer OS release has no mechanical impact on data-center power conversion, and the 10-K evidence is strong. However, the what_kills criterion explicitly flags 'hyperscaler capex digestion narrative gaining traction'—a material risk that could already be reflected in the -5.45% move and 12.45% five-day fade on 0.67x volume. If large customers are already signaling slowdowns in their infrastructure spending (a near-term macro reality), the stock may be repricing on visibility, not sympathy. Haiku does not assess the current state of capex guidance from TSMC, Meta, or other AEIS end-markets.

entries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's new 'AI Mode' on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
entries.thesis: Tyler sells court records, municipal ERP, and government workflow systems with multi-year procurement cycles, RFP-based sales, and decades-long client tenure. A Meta consumer AI feature has zero realistic path to displacing system-of-record software for courts and cities — no municipal CIO replaces a case management system with an LLM. The selloff is pure SaaS-AI thematic sympathy.
entries.what_kills: Government RFPs explicitly favoring AI-native competitors over Tyler; large municipal customer non-renewals; concurrent guidance cut.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Conf 4, OVERDONE, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Tyler Technologies: sells court records, municipal ERP, government workflow systems with multi-year procurement cycles and RFP-based sales. Meta consumer AI Mode has zero plausible path to displacing system-of-record software for courts and municipalities. Five-day move -4.59% before today's -3.01% suggests some existing pressure, but core thesis is sound—government IT is structural, not exposed to consumer AI feature releases. Filings evidence clean: AI discussed as capability and cybersecurity risk, not as existential threat. Part of Meta AI Mode basket (3+ flags from same trigger); basket rule applies → exploratory tier.

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day move of -4.59% followed by today's -3.01% shows a two-day downtrend that could signal deteriorating SaaS multiples rather than isolated Meta AI Mode sympathy.
red_team.critique: Haiku argues government-vertical SaaS has a structural moat impervious to consumer AI releases, and that is credible. However, the two-day sequence (-4.59% over five days, -3.01% today) suggests continuous selling pressure, not a one-day sympathy flush. If the broader SaaS sector is experiencing multiple compression on generalist AI risk (even unfounded), TYL could be caught in a sector-wide rotation rather than a rational false-alarm. Haiku does not address whether TYL trades as part of a SaaS index or thematic basket that is itself under pressure independent of the Meta catalyst.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: A consumer OS release adding Gemini features to Android phones has zero mechanical connection to photomask demand. If anything, more AI compute = more advanced-node chip designs = more photomask demand. The selloff is pure thematic semi-sympathy tape with the sign flipped — Photronics is a beneficiary of AI capex, not a victim of a consumer mobile feature release.
entries.what_kills: An actual cyclical downturn in semi capex unrelated to today's trigger; loss of a top-two customer; further Section 232 / China export restrictions that materially reduce PLAB's addressable Asia revenue (82% of total).
entries.reasoning: PLAB already held in portfolio (day 6, +4.14%). Flag is a re-flag on the same position from a different trigger (Android 17 Gemini vs. earlier Anthropic guardrails). Screen 1 protocol does not support ADD; thesis is intact and position is recovering. No new trade warranted.

entries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: A consumer mobile OS adding Gemini features doesn't touch payroll-tax filing, ACH funds movement, multi-jurisdictional employment law, or PAYC's bank-of-record relationship with employee comp. The moat is regulatory complexity and integration depth, not the user-facing UI. PAYC was caught in broad SaaS sympathy tape; mobile-OS AI integration is not the relevant threat vector.
entries.what_kills: Acceleration in clients reducing headcount due to AI (PAYC's own stated risk — they bill per employee); a vertical AI-payroll competitor showing real traction; PAYC client churn data turning negative.
entries.reasoning: Conf 3 (at threshold), threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Paycom's core moat is regulated payroll-tax compliance and ACH banking integration, not a chatbot interface. However, 10-K explicitly names headcount reduction as a risk ('since PAYC charges per-employee') and filings evidence is thin on the 'why this is isolated.' Confidence exactly at threshold with 'minimal' (not 'none') threat means borderline. Five-day already negative (-5.15%) before Android trigger—indicates broader SaaS weakness, not sympathy fade in isolation. SKIP and monitor for thesis clarity on next data point.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: DOCN's SMB/indie-developer cloud is orthogonal to a consumer Android+Gemini release. Their AI inference offerings (GPU droplets, GenAI platform) actually align them with the trend. The mechanical link from 'Google ships Gemini in Android' to 'DigitalOcean's SMB cloud subscriptions decline' is nonexistent.
entries.what_kills: Hyperscaler price-cuts on small instances making DOCN's pricing edge less meaningful; AI/ML revenue traction failing to materialize in next quarter; SMB churn acceleration.
entries.reasoning: Conf 3, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. DigitalOcean's SMB cloud and AI/ML product offerings are orthogonal to Android+Gemini. However, five-day move is only +3.48% before today's -3.92% drop—modest sympathy footprint suggests limited panic to fade. Volume is light (0.52x). Confidence is threshold (3) and filings evidence (AI listed as growth opportunity, not threat) is thin on quantitative conviction. SKIP—this is a valid fade candidate but not strong enough conviction to size exploratory.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: A consumer mobile OS Gemini integration has no plausible mechanical link to enterprise warehouse-management system demand. WMS is deeply embedded with physical material handling, ERP, and supply-chain processes — switching cost, not the AI text layer, is the moat. The five-day was already negative going into today; the broader application-software sympathy fade caught MANH in the basket.
entries.what_kills: A real AI-native WMS or OMS competitor demonstrating large-customer wins; retail vertical capex pullback (MANH's largest end market); guidance cut tied to deal-cycle elongation.
entries.reasoning: Conf 3, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Manhattan Associates' WMS moat is switching cost and integration depth, not the AI text layer. However, five-day change is already negative (-4.87%) before today—market was repricing application software broadly, not sympathy-fading one trigger. Confidence at threshold (3) and thesis requires some parsing of 'general AI risk' language in filings. SKIP due to insufficient confidence + pre-existing negative momentum suggesting broader sector pressure, not isolated sympathy fade.

entries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: HCAT has plenty of real issues (DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, multiple restructurings, end-market funding cuts) but a consumer Android Gemini release is not one of them. HIPAA, BAA requirements, and EHR integration depth prevent general-purpose AI agents from touching provider data. The drop is sympathy-fade on top of an already-pressured name. Confidence intentionally limited because HCAT has genuine, name-specific issues that make this less clean.
entries.what_kills: Further Medicaid funding cuts pressuring health-system IT spend; client losses to Epic/Oracle accelerating; Ignite migration causing more revenue churn than guided.
entries.reasoning: Conf 2 (below min_buy_confidence of 3), threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Health Catalyst has genuine, name-specific headwinds (DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, restructurings, Medicaid funding pressure) that confound the sympathy-fade thesis. Flag author explicitly notes 'HCAT has plenty of real issues' and confidence was 'intentionally limited.' Android Gemini is not the true catalyst here. SKIP due to below-threshold confidence and broken thesis (real impairment, not sympathy).

entries.name: Stride, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's new 'AI Mode' on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
entries.thesis: Stride's revenue is contractually tied to per-pupil state funding for accredited virtual public schools — parents enroll children for diplomas, transcripts, and state-funded education, none of which a Meta Facebook AI feature can deliver. The sympathy-fade premise ('AI tutors replace edtech') is the wrong frame; Stride is a regulated school operator, not a tutoring app. The move appears to be an extreme overreaction conflating Stride with consumer edtech apps.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of state legislatures cutting virtual charter funding, charter non-renewals, or material enrollment declines in the next data release; a concurrent negative catalyst we missed.
entries.reasoning: LRN already held in portfolio (day 6, -13.81%, position trimmed above). Same name is appearing as fresh flag from Meta AI Mode trigger (not Android). This is a re-flag/pile-on signal on a position being trimmed. No new BUY warranted after TRIM decision.
